texto: Humans don’t just kill to survive. Sometimes, they kill out of rage. And they don’t just eat to survive; sometimes, they eat when their belly is already full. They are violent and greedy. They aren’t like any of the other beasts in the forest; they want to own it all.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark Van Steenwyk: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Humans don’t just kill to survive. Sometimes, they kill out of rage. And they don’t just eat to s…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, animals, vegan pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vegan.

frase_original: Humans don’t just kill to survive. Sometimes, they kill out of rage. And they don’t just eat to survive; sometimes, they eat when their belly is already full. They are violent and greedy. They aren’t like any of the other beasts in the forest; they want to own it all.
